hero.title: Protecting Canadian
hero.body: ElevateIP is a national program empowering Canada's most promising startups with expert intellectual property guidance delivered through a network of trusted regional accelerators and incubators.

valueProps.heading: Where IP Strategy Meets Startup Growth
valueProps.body: {elevateip}, with the support of {ised}, works with Business Accelerators and Incubators (BAIs) across Canada to deliver professional, stage-appropriate IP services to the startups they support.

values.title: IP Strategy First
values.body: We help Canadian startups understand, develop, and protect their most valuable assets: their ideas.

values.title: Growth-Stage Ready
values.body: From seed to scale, our programs align IP strategy with your funding and commercialization milestones.

values.title: Nationwide Coverage
values.body: A unified network of regional BAIs spans every province and territory, so every founder has a local expert.

regionalRouter.heading: Your Regional IP Partner Is Waiting
regionalRouter.body: Select your province or territory below to be connected with your dedicated regional program and local IP specialists.
regionalRouter.helperText: Please select the province your company is registered in.

resources.heading: Nationally Available Tools & Resources
resources.body: No matter where you are in Canada, these resources are freely available to every founder in the ElevateIP ecosystem.

items.title: IP Fundamentals for Startups
items.desc: An introduction to patents, trademarks, trade secrets, and copyright for early-stage companies.

items.title: Build an IP Strategy
items.desc: Use the Canadian Intellectual Property Office to help you get started to build an IP Strategy.

items.title: National IP Webinar Series
items.desc: Monthly live sessions with IP professionals covering commercialization, licensing, and enforcement.

items.title: Service Provider Directory
items.desc: Connect with an IP advisor from our national network tailored to your sector and stage.

stories.tagline: Without ElevateIP, we might have had to choose between protecting our innovations or investing in product improvements. This program allowed us to do both.
stories.summary: Founded by University of Waterloo graduates, Alchemy scaled their advanced protective film technologies into the high-stakes global defence sector by leveraging a comprehensive IP landscape analysis.

stories.tagline: Securing the future of bio-manufacturing: How 3D BioFibR partnered with ElevateIP to build a bulletproof international patent strategy.
stories.summary: Halifax-based biotech leader 3D BioFibR leveraged the ElevateIP program to map and secure a robust international patent strategy for their proprietary, automated collagen-fiber manufacturing technology. This critical support empowered the company to confidently scale their high-value bio-manufacturing innovations globally while protecting their core commercial assets.

facts.text: Canadian SMEs holding registered IP rights are

footer.disclaimer: {elevateip} is a federally funded, national project to help business accelerators and incubators (BAIs) provide the tools Canadian startups need to understand, strategically manage and leverage their intellectual property.

experts.heading: National IP Experts
experts.subheading: Vetted intellectual property professionals connected through the ElevateIP national network. Find the right expert for your stage and region.
experts.searchPlaceholder: Search by firm name, specialty, or city…
experts.noExpertsBody: No firms match your current filters. Try adjusting your search or broadening your criteria.

intake.heading: National Service Provider Intake
intake.body: A centralized intake form for IP professionals and service providers to join the national ElevateIP network is currently in development.

expertProfile.disclaimer: This listing is provided for informational purposes only and does not constitute an endorsement of any individual service provider.

partnerLogoWall.heading: Our National Delivery Partners
partnerLogoWall.body: Supporting startups across every region of Canada.

partners.heading: Ecosystem Partners
partners.body: Connecting Canadian startups with the national network of intellectual property support organizations.

eventCalendar.heading: Upcoming IP Events
eventCalendar.body: Workshops, webinars, and clinics from Canada's ElevateIP partner network — approved and curated for startup founders.
eventCalendar.submitCta: Are you an ElevateIP Partner? Submit an event here
eventCalendar.noEventsBody: There are no approved events scheduled right now. Check back soon, or submit yours above.

submitEventModal.title: Submit an Event

title_fr.label: Event Title (French)

submitEventModal.successBody: Your event has been received and is pending review. We'll approve it shortly.
submitEventModal.disclaimer: Submitted events are reviewed before appearing on the calendar.

intro.heading: Introduction
intro.p1: This Privacy Policy applies to the ElevateIP National Portal (the “Portal”), operated on behalf of the ElevateIP Delivery Partners who deliver ElevateIP, a federally funded initiative supported by Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED). It governs how information is collected, used, and disclosed when you access or interact with this Portal. By using the Portal, you acknowledge and agree to the practices described herein.
intro.p2pre: ElevateIP Partners are committed to handling all personal and organizational information with care, discretion, and in accordance with applicable Canadian privacy legislation, including the
intro.p2law2: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

collection.heading: Information We Collect
collection.voluntaryIntro: When you interact with features of the Portal — such as selecting your province or territory, submitting an event, or reaching out via contact forms — you may provide information including:
voluntaryItems: Province or territory of business registration
Event details submitted for calendar consideration
collection.metaIntro: Like most web properties, the Portal may automatically collect certain technical metadata when you visit, including:
metaItems: Pages visited and time spent on the Portal
A language preference cookie (ELEVATEIP_LOCALE) to remember your selected language between visits
collection.cookieNote: You may configure your browser to decline cookies. Doing so will not impair access to the Portal’s core content.

dataRouting.heading: Data Routing & Usage
dataRouting.intro: Information submitted through the Portal is used solely to deliver the ElevateIP Partners programs’ national service mandate. Specifically:
dataRouting.callout: Information submitted regarding your province of registration is utilized to route your inquiry to the appropriate regional delivery partner. Aggregated, non-identifiable metrics may be shared with Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED) for program evaluation purposes.
dataRouting.closing: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to third parties. Information is not used for commercial marketing purposes and will not be disclosed beyond the scope described in this Policy without your explicit consent, except as required by law.

security.heading: Security
security.intro: ElevateIP Partners take the security of your information seriously. We employ industry-standard technical and organizational safeguards, including:
items: SSL/TLS encryption for all data transmitted between your browser and the Portal
Hosting on Vercel’s enterprise-grade, SOC 2-compliant infrastructure with automatic DDoS mitigation
Role-based access controls limiting data access to authorized program personnel only
Regular security reviews and dependency audits
security.closing: While we implement robust safeguards, no method of transmission over the internet is entirely secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security but are committed to continuous improvement of our protective measures.

retention.heading: Data Retention
retention.body: We retain personal information only as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected or as required by applicable law. Aggregated, anonymized analytics data may be retained for longer periods to support ongoing program evaluation by ISED.

changes.heading: Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically to reflect changes in program operations, technology, or applicable law. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page indicates when the Policy was last revised. Continued use of the Portal following any update constitutes acceptance of the revised Policy.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.pre: If you have questions or concerns regarding this Privacy Policy or the handling of your information, please contact

commitment.heading: Our Commitment
commitment.p1: ElevateIP Delivery Partners are committed to providing a digital environment that is accessible, inclusive, and equitable for every founder, partner, and stakeholder who engages with the national portal, regardless of ability, assistive technology, or device.
commitment.p2: We recognize that accessibility is not a feature — it is a fundamental requirement of a public-facing national program. Our goal is to ensure that all users, including those who rely on screen readers, keyboard navigation, or other assistive technologies, can access the full breadth of information and tools available through this portal.

conformance.heading: Conformance Standard
conformance.intro: The ElevateIP National Portal targets conformance with the
conformance.standard: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1, Level AA
conformance.mid: , published by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C). These guidelines define how to make web content more accessible to people with disabilities and form the foundation of accessibility legislation across Canada and internationally.
conformance.closing: Where full conformance has not yet been achieved, we are actively working to remediate known gaps in accordance with our ongoing accessibility roadmap.

features.heading: Implemented Accessibility Features
features.intro: The following measures have been implemented across the portal to support WCAG 2.1 Level AA conformance:

items.title: High-Contrast Typography
items.body: All body text and interactive elements are rendered against backgrounds with sufficient colour contrast ratios. Primary text uses our deep plum palette against a white background, meeting or exceeding the 4.5:1 contrast ratio required at Level AA.

items.title: Semantic HTML Structure
items.body: The portal is built using semantic landmark elements — <header>, <main>, <nav>, <section>, <footer>, and <aside> — enabling screen readers to accurately communicate the page hierarchy and allow users to navigate directly to content regions of interest.

items.title: Keyboard-Navigable Components
items.body: All interactive components, including the province and territory selection dropdown, navigation menus, search inputs, and call-to-action buttons, are fully operable using keyboard controls alone. Focus states are visually distinct and compliant with WCAG 2.4.7.

items.title: Descriptive Link Text & ARIA Labels
items.body: Links and icon-only buttons include descriptive accessible names or ARIA labels, ensuring that users navigating via screen readers receive meaningful context without relying on visual cues.

items.title: Responsive & Scalable Layout
items.body: The portal layout is fully responsive and supports text scaling up to 200% without loss of content or functionality, in accordance with WCAG 1.4.4.

items.title: Language Declaration
items.body: The HTML lang attribute is programmatically set to reflect the active language (English or French), enabling assistive technologies to apply the correct pronunciation and linguistic rules.

limitations.heading: Known Limitations
limitations.body: While we strive for full conformance, some third-party content integrated into the portal — including external links, embedded media, and partner-submitted resources — may not meet all WCAG 2.1 Level AA criteria. We are not responsible for the accessibility of content hosted on third-party platforms, but we encourage our partners to adopt equivalent standards.

feedback.heading: Feedback & Reporting Barriers
feedback.intro: We welcome feedback from all users. If you encounter an accessibility barrier on this portal — or if any content or feature is not accessible to you — please contact us directly so we can address the issue promptly.
feedback.calloutPost: with a description of the barrier you encountered, the page URL, and the assistive technology or browser you were using. We aim to respond to accessibility inquiries within five business days.
